name: architecture-decision-record
description: Use this skill when documenting significant architectural decisions. Provides ADR templates following the Nygard format with sections for context, decision, consequences, and alternatives. Helps teams maintain architectural memory and rationale for backend systems, API designs, database choices, and infrastructure decisions.
capabilities: Generate ADR documents using Nygard format templates
Document decision context, alternatives, and trade-offs
Create YAML frontmatter for ADR metadata management
Follow review checklists for ADR quality assurance
Model real-world examples of ADRs for common scenarios
Track ADR status lifecycle from proposed to deprecated
author: AI Agent Hub
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown templates and guidance. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file system access. All static scanner findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown syntax and documentation text as code/security issues. Prior audit correctly identified this as safe with zero risk factors.
valueStatement: Teams struggle to remember why architectural decisions were made, leading to repeated debates and rework. This skill provides templates and examples for creating Architecture Decision Records that capture context, alternatives, and trade-offs so teams maintain architectural memory for future reference.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files automatically
Does not integrate with version control systems
Does not generate diagrams or visual artifacts
Does not enforce ADR compliance in workflows

useCases.title: Document system architecture
useCases.description: Record major architectural choices with full context for team reference and stakeholder alignment

useCases.title: Establish technical standards
useCases.description: Create records of technology selection decisions including database, framework, and infrastructure choices

useCases.title: Capture decision rationale
useCases.description: Document why certain approaches were chosen so future team members understand the reasoning

promptTemplates.title: Database Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an ADR to document our database selection decision between PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and DynamoDB. Include the context of our microservices architecture, requirements for ACID compliance, and comparison of trade-offs.

promptTemplates.title: API Versioning
promptTemplates.prompt: Document our API versioning strategy decision. Compare URL path versioning versus header-based versioning versus query parameter versioning. Explain why we chose URL path versioning.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an ADR for migrating from a monolith to microservices. Include the strangler fig pattern approach, phase-by-phase implementation plan, and honest assessment of consequences.

promptTemplates.title: Technology Stack
promptTemplates.prompt: Document our technology stack selection for a new backend service. Cover programming language, framework, database, and deployment infrastructure choices with clear rationale for each decision.

bestPractices: Write ADRs in present tense as historical records that capture decisions at the moment they were made
Document at least two alternatives with honest pros and cons to demonstrate thorough evaluation
Keep ADRs immutable after acceptance - create new ADRs to supersede rather than editing existing ones
antiPatterns: Using vague language like "we might consider using" instead of clear decisions like "we will adopt"
Only listing positive consequences without acknowledging costs, risks, and trade-offs honestly
Skipping alternatives or presenting only obviously inferior options as strawmen

faq.question: What is the difference between this and a design document?
faq.answer: ADRs focus specifically on the why behind decisions, not implementation details. They capture trade-offs and alternatives considered.

faq.question: How many ADRs should a project have?
faq.answer: Only create ADRs for significant decisions that affect multiple components, require team alignment, or involve trade-offs.

faq.question: Can ADRs be integrated with my codebase?
faq.answer: Store ADRs in /docs/adr/ or /architecture/decisions/ alongside code. Use the YAML frontmatter for tooling integration.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only generates documentation templates. No data is sent anywhere or stored externally.

faq.question: Why should I document alternatives I rejected?
faq.answer: Documenting alternatives prevents repeated debates. Future team members can understand why certain approaches were considered and rejected.

faq.question: How does this compare to ADRs in other tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides the Nygard format templates and examples. Integration with specific tools depends on your workflow.
